# Set-Detail Navigation & Code Generator Fix — Walkthrough

## Summary

Fixed the set-detail navigation bugs **and** the root cause in the code generator that produced the incorrect code.

## Changes

### 1. Generator Fix: `AngularServiceGenerator.cs`
[AngularServiceGenerator.cs](file:///g:/source/repos/Scheduler/CodeGenerationCore/AngularServiceGenerator.cs)

**Root cause:** The `Revive{entity}` method was generated by iterating `tablesThatLinkHere` (unique table names), while all other generated code — class fields, observables, `clearAllLazyCaches()`, and `Get{X}For{Entity}()` — iterate `foreignKeysThatLinkHere` (per-FK). When a table has multiple FKs back to the same parent (e.g., `LegoSetSubset` → `LegoSet` via both `parentLegoSetId` and `childLegoSetId`), the Revive method generated one generic cache name instead of two FK-specific names.

**Fix:** Replaced both `foreach (string tableName in tablesThatLinkHere)` loops with `foreach (Database.Table.ForeignKey fk in foreignKeysThatLinkHere)` loops, using the same simple/complex FK naming logic present throughout the rest of the generator. Also fixed the `RowCount` query parameter to use `fk.field.name` instead of always assuming `{entity}Id`.

render_diffs(file:///g:/source/repos/Scheduler/CodeGenerationCore/AngularServiceGenerator.cs)

---

### 2. Generated Output Fixes (applied directly)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| [lego-set.service.ts](file:///g:/source/repos/Scheduler/BMC/BMC.Client/src/app/bmc-data-services/lego-set.service.ts) | `ReviveLegoSet`: split single `_legoSetSubsets` cache into `_legoSetSubsetParentLegoSets` + `_legoSetSubsetChildLegoSets` with correct observables |
| [set-detail.component.ts](file:///g:/source/repos/Scheduler/BMC/BMC.Client/src/app/components/set-detail/set-detail.component.ts) | Changed subset getter from `LegoSetSubsetChildLegoSets` → `LegoSetSubsetParentLegoSets`; added minifig diagnostic log |

## Verification

- **CodeGenerationCore build:** ✅ 0 errors
- **BMC.Client production build:** ✅ Passed
